rdfs:label: PhysWorld Military Radar Ontology (PWMO-RADAR)
rdfs:comment: The PhysWorldLM Master Radar Ontology. Canonical engineering-grade schema covering every radar class, component, RF characteristic, signal processing block, countermeasure, vulnerability, logistic concept, relationship type, and worldwide inventory. Version 1.0.0.

UpperTaxonomy.rdfs:label: Radar
UpperTaxonomy.skos:definition: A Radio Detection And Ranging system that uses electromagnetic radiation to detect, locate, track, and classify objects by measuring reflected signals. Encompasses all military, dual-use, and experimental radar variants.
UpperTaxonomy.pwmo:physics: Emits electromagnetic pulses or continuous waveforms; detects backscatter from targets; measures range (time-of-flight), velocity (Doppler shift), angle (beam position), and in advanced systems: polarimetric and coherent phase information.
UpperTaxonomy.pwmo:fundamentalEquation: P_r = (P_t * G_t * G_r * lambda^2 * sigma) / ((4*pi)^3 * R^4 * L)

AirborneRadar.rdfs:label: Airborne Radar
AirborneRadar.skos:definition: Radar systems installed on manned or unmanned aircraft, helicopters, airships, or UAVs. Subject to size, weight, power (SWaP) constraints and vibration environments.

GroundRadar.rdfs:label: Ground-Based Radar
GroundRadar.skos:definition: Radar systems deployed on land, including fixed installations, mobile platforms, vehicle-mounted systems, and man-portable units.
pwmo:disadvantages: Limited elevation coverage for ground clutter

NavalRadar.rdfs:label: Naval Radar
NavalRadar.skos:definition: Radar systems installed on surface ships, submarines, and unmanned surface/underwater vehicles. Must operate in high-salt, high-humidity marine environments.

SpaceRadar.rdfs:label: Space-Based Radar
SpaceRadar.skos:definition: Radar systems deployed on satellites or space stations, providing global coverage and persistent surveillance from low-earth, medium-earth, or geostationary orbit.
pwmo:disadvantages: Limited resolution vs airborne at same geometry

PassiveRadar.rdfs:label: Passive Radar
PassiveRadar.skos:definition: Radar systems that do not transmit their own signal but instead exploit third-party transmissions (broadcast, cellular, DVB-T, DAB, WiFi, etc.) as illuminators of opportunity to detect and track targets.

BistaticRadar.rdfs:label: Bistatic Radar
BistaticRadar.skos:definition: Radar in which the transmitter and receiver are separated by a distance comparable to the expected target range, enabling detection of targets with low monostatic RCS.
BistaticRadar.pwmo:bistatic_RCS_advantage: Stealth aircraft optimized to minimize monostatic RCS may have large bistatic RCS at certain geometries (specular return)

MultistaticRadar.rdfs:label: Multistatic Radar
MultistaticRadar.skos:definition: Network of multiple spatially diverse transmitters and receivers providing cooperative target detection with superior coverage, accuracy, and stealth aircraft detection probability.
MultistaticRadar.pwmo:keyMetric: Multistatic RCS averaging across multiple aspect angles defeats stealth coatings optimized for single-look geometries

MechanicalScanRadar.rdfs:label: Mechanically Scanned Radar
MechanicalScanRadar.skos:definition: Radar using physical rotation or mechanical beam steering. Includes parabolic dish and slotted array antennas driven by servo motors.

PESARadar.rdfs:label: Passive Electronically Scanned Array (PESA)
PESARadar.skos:definition: Radar with a phased array antenna using electronic phase shifters driven by a single central transmitter to steer the beam electronically without mechanical movement.
pwmo:keyComponents: Ferrite or PIN Diode Phase Shifters
pwmo:disadvantages: Single transmitter is single point of failure

AESARadar.rdfs:label: Active Electronically Scanned Array (AESA)
AESARadar.skos:definition: Radar where each antenna element has its own Transmit/Receive (TR) module containing an individual solid-state power amplifier, phase shifter, and low-noise amplifier. Enables simultaneous multiple beams, high reliability, extreme agility, and LPI.
AESARadar.pwmo:gracefulDegradation: AESA maintains reduced but functional performance when up to 25-30% of TR modules fail

HybridScanRadar.rdfs:label: Hybrid Scan Radar
HybridScanRadar.skos:definition: Radar combining mechanical rotation or tilt with electronic scanning in one or more axes. Common configuration: mechanical azimuth rotation with electronic elevation scanning.
pwmo:disadvantages: Mechanical rotation limits azimuth update rate

SoftwareDefinedRadar.rdfs:label: Software Defined Radar (SDR)
SoftwareDefinedRadar.skos:definition: Radar where waveform generation, signal processing, and operational modes are defined and reconfigured in software rather than hardware, enabling rapid mission adaptation and multi-function operation.

CognitiveRadar.rdfs:label: Cognitive Radar
CognitiveRadar.skos:definition: Radar incorporating closed-loop perception-action cycles: continuously learns from the environment, adapts waveforms, processing, and beam management to optimize performance against evolving threats and interference.
CognitiveRadar.pwmo:corePrinciple: Bayesian inference or reinforcement learning to adapt TX waveform based on RX observations

QuantumRadar.rdfs:label: Quantum Radar
QuantumRadar.skos:definition: Theoretical/experimental radar exploiting quantum entanglement (quantum illumination) or photon number states to detect stealth targets at very low signal-to-noise ratios, potentially defeating radar-absorbing materials.
QuantumRadar.pwmo:theoreticalAdvantage: 6 dB SNR advantage over classical radar in quantum-noise-limited regime
pwmo:currentLimitations: Range limited to meters in current implementations
Cannot yet scale to operational radar ranges
pwmo:claimedPrograms: China CETC quantum radar claims (2016, disputed)
QuantumRadar.pwmo:consensus: No operational quantum radar exists; claims should be treated with extreme skepticism

EarlyWarningRadar.rdfs:label: Early Warning Radar (EWR)
EarlyWarningRadar.skos:definition: Long-range radar providing maximum detection range and early cueing of incoming threats including aircraft, cruise missiles, and ballistic missiles. Typically operates in VHF/UHF/L-band for long range and counter-stealth capability.
EarlyWarningRadar.pwmo:primaryMission: Maximum unambiguous detection range; cueing downstream fire-control systems; national/theater air picture generation
EarlyWarningRadar.pwmo:counterStealthCapability: VHF/UHF wavelengths (1-2m) approach resonance region for stealth aircraft with wingspan ~10-20m, degrading RAM effectiveness

FireControlRadar.rdfs:label: Fire Control Radar (FCR)
FireControlRadar.skos:definition: Precision tracking radar providing continuous, high-accuracy target position data to weapon systems for engagement. Requires high update rate, precise angular tracking, and range/velocity measurement accuracy.
FireControlRadar.pwmo:primaryMission: Single or multi-target precise tracking for weapon guidance; illumination; terminal homing support

CounterBatteryRadar.rdfs:label: Counter-Battery / Weapon Locating Radar
CounterBatteryRadar.skos:definition: Radar system detecting projectiles in flight (shells, rockets, mortars, missiles) and back-computing their launch point to enable counter-fire. Also predicts impact point for force protection.
CounterBatteryRadar.pwmo:operatingPrinciple: Detects small-RCS projectiles in clutter; multi-point tracking extrapolation to solve ballistic trajectory; least-squares or Kalman filter back-extrapolation to origin

SARRadar.rdfs:label: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR)
SARRadar.skos:definition: Imaging radar exploiting platform motion to synthesize a very large virtual antenna aperture, producing high-resolution ground images regardless of weather and day/night conditions.
SARRadar.pwmo:fundamentalPrinciple: Coherent integration of returns across platform travel distance L_SA; azimuth resolution d_az ≈ D/2 independent of range (for focused SAR)

pwmo:imagingModes.Stripmap: Beam fixed relative to platform; continuous strip image along track
pwmo:imagingModes.Spotlight: Beam steered to illuminate fixed area longer; higher azimuth resolution
pwmo:imagingModes.ScanSAR: Beam steps across multiple strips; wide swath at reduced resolution
pwmo:imagingModes.TOPSAR: Terrain Observation by Progressive Scans; burst mode wide-swath, uniform azimuth ambiguity
pwmo:imagingModes.Inverse_SAR_ISAR: Target moves, radar fixed; ship and aircraft imaging

pwmo:interferometric.InSAR: Phase difference between two passes; terrain height map (DEM generation)
pwmo:interferometric.DInSAR: Differential InSAR; ground deformation measurement (mm accuracy)

SARRadar.pwmo:changeDetection: Comparison of multi-temporal SAR images to detect changes in terrain, infrastructure, or military disposition
SARRadar.pwmo:applicationToStructDiff: SAR image domain; speckle noise is multiplicative Gamma-distributed; despeckling is core preprocessing task. Connects directly to StructDiff SAR-DDPM framework.

GMTIRadar.rdfs:label: Ground Moving Target Indicator (GMTI) Radar
GMTIRadar.skos:definition: Airborne or space-based radar detecting and tracking slow-moving ground targets (vehicles, personnel) against strong ground clutter returns using Doppler processing and space-time adaptive processing (STAP).
GMTIRadar.pwmo:keyProcessing: STAP (Space-Time Adaptive Processing) nulls clutter while preserving slow-moving target signals; minimum detectable velocity (MDV) is key metric
GMTIRadar.pwmo:challengesInSAR: Slow targets smear in SAR image; GMTI processing separates them from clutter

UASCounterRadar.rdfs:label: Counter-UAS Radar
UASCounterRadar.skos:definition: Radar optimized to detect, classify, and track small unmanned aircraft systems (UAS/drones) which present very low RCS, potentially fly nap-of-earth, and may operate in swarms.

HypersonicTrackingRadar.rdfs:label: Hypersonic Missile Tracking Radar
HypersonicTrackingRadar.skos:definition: Radar designed to detect, track, and support intercept of hypersonic glide vehicles (HGV) and hypersonic cruise missiles traveling at Mach 5+, characterized by maneuvering flight, plasma sheath, and very short intercept timelines.
pwmo:challenges: High closing velocity requires very fast beam updates
HypersonicTrackingRadar.pwmo:maturityLevel: Active development (2020s); no confirmed fully operational system
